title: WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS - 211info
text: COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workshops on basic weatherization, DIY insulation, and basic lead poisoning prevention. Qualified participants also receive a free kit of materials including items such as reusable vinyl storm window kits, door weather stripping, and pipe insulation. Materials are also available for purchase. Also offers in-home repairs and energy efficiency upgrades. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Workshops: Unrestricted, but free supplies are restricted to households earning no more than 80% median family income In-home weatherization and small safety repairs: Age 55 and older and adults with disabilities earning no more than 80% median family income Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:30am-5pm Workshops typically late September to early February, depending on weather and funding availability Email (503) 284-6827 108 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 284-6827 Main phone Main phone (503) 284-6827 ext 108 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information or to register for a workshop FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, Russian, Vietnamese, Arabic, Burmese, Rohingya, Karenic languages, Nepali, Simplified Chinese, Somali, Zomi AREA SERVED: Multnomah County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 20 stops at 28th and E Burnside Street Services: Home Maintenance and Minor Repair Services * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home Maintenance and Minor Repair Services * Older Adults Lead Information Subject Specific Public Awareness/Education * Energy Conservation Issues Weatherization Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - DIY SUMMER WEATHERIZATION WORKSHOPS UTILITIES - LOW INCOME COMMUNITY SOLAR If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT
